target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched (shared gate functions — model-capability resolution, OAuth label, credential fingerprint, live-agent probes, model alias detection — extended via a single `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` helper to recognise both backend ids; every gate continues to return identical values for `claude-cli`). `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: fix: promote serialized tool calls in harness
target.body:
## Summary

- Promotes standalone serialized text tool calls in the embedded runner into structured `toolCall` blocks so they execute through the normal tool lifecycle instead of leaking into chat.
- Extends the shared plaintext tool-call parser/buffer handling for XML parameter payloads, case/alias matching, interleaved partials, cancellation, terminal errors, and whitespace-sensitive arguments.
- Keeps provider compat buffering aligned so provider wrappers do not stream raw serialized tool text before final conversion.
- Restores the cumulative XMLish payload cap for promotion while still stripping oversized serialized tool-call blocks from user-visible text.

## Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A["Provider stream emits assistant text"] --> B{"Standalone serialized tool call?"}
  B -- "No" --> C["Stream normal assistant text"]
  B -- "Maybe" --> D["Buffer partial text instead of displaying it"]
  D --> E{"Terminal message is only serialized tool-call text?"}
  E -- "No" --> F["Scrub serialized block from visible text"]
  E -- "Yes" --> G["Parse allowed tool name and arguments"]
  G --> H{"Within execution payload cap?"}
  H -- "No" --> F
  H -- "Yes" --> I["Promote to structured toolCall"]
  I --> J["Run through normal tool lifecycle"]
  J --> K["Return tool result to model"]
```

## Comment follow-up

- Restored cumulative XMLish `maxPayloadBytes` enforcement across multiple parameters.
- Split execution parsing from final text stripping so over-cap serialized tool calls are not promoted, but are still removed from outbound/user-visible text.
- Rebased onto current `upstream/main` and picked up the unrelated CI stabilization commits that fixed old-base check failures.
- Kept interleaved non-text events buffered in source order until a possible serialized tool prefix is classified, so real tool calls are scrubbed/promoted and false positives flush in original stream order.
- Preserved partial `contentIndex` stability by keeping empty text placeholders only when removing a hidden buffered text block would shift the current event index.
- Restored reclassified mixed-text scrubbing for terminal error partials.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts --threads=1`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts --reporter=verbose` - 3 files, 115 tests passed.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings.
- Current-head live terminal proof listed below.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: standalone serialized tool-call text is converted to structured `toolCall` blocks at the harness layer and withheld from streamed chat surfaces while pending; oversized serialized XMLish tool-call blocks are rejected for execution promotion but still stripped from user-visible text; interleaved partials keep ordering and `contentIndex` stability while the possible tool text is classified.

Real environment tested: Local macOS terminal against current PR head `30da34cf9e529004758227dd57924ce446185920`, with the built bundled plugin runtime (`OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=$PWD/dist/extensions`) and a temporary minimal OpenClaw config using the existing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` auth profile. This is current-head terminal proof; Testbox-through-Crabbox and direct AWS Crabbox attempts were blocked before checkout by external auth/quota errors.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
tmp_config=$(mktemp -t openclaw-live-pr87093.XXXXXX.json)
cat > "$tmp_config" <<'JSON'
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "workspace": "/tmp/openclaw-live-pr87093-workspace",
      "model": {
        "primary": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
      }
    },
    "list": [{ "id": "main" }]
  },
  "models": { "providers": {} }
}
JSON
CI=1 \
NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 \
OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR="$PWD/dist/extensions" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmp_config" \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_SMOKE=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_PROVIDERS=openai-codex \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MAX_MODELS=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_STEP_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MODEL_TIMEOUT_MS=600000 \
node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-models.profiles.live.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Current-head live console output, with local paths and host identifiers omitted:

```text
HEAD=30da34cf9e529004758227dd57924ce446185920
[live] [all-models] discover candidates providers=openai-codex
[live] [all-models] wanted=1 total=6
[live] [all-models] candidates=1 skipped=0
[live] [all-models] selection=high-signal
[gateway] agent model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] ready
[live] [all-models] running 1 models (thinking=low)
[live] [all-models] 1/1 openai-codex/gpt-5.5: prompt
[live] [all-models] 1/1 openai-codex/gpt-5.5: tool-read
The JavaScript event loop runs one macrotask at a time, such as a script, timer callback, or I/O callback. After each macrotask finishes, it drains the microtask queue completely, before rendering and moving to the next macrotask.
[live] [all-models] 1/1 openai-codex/gpt-5.5: tool-only regression
nonceA=5de2e37a-6812-496e-bbfc-ee476cb968fc
nonceB=a28bf551-664e-474d-b972-dc3406b6c279
[agent/embedded] empty response detected: provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 - retrying 1/1 with visible-answer continuation
nonceA=5de2e37a-6812-496e-bbfc-ee476cb968fc
nonceB=a28bf551-664e-474d-b972-dc3406b6c279
[live] [all-models] 1/1 openai-codex/gpt-5.5: model: completed after 53s
[live] [all-models] 1/1 openai-codex/gpt-5.5: done

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  68 passed (68)
Duration  63.94s
```

Observed result after fix: The live gateway path reached the embedded runner, exercised the `tool-read` and `tool-only regression` phases, read the nonce file through the tool lifecycle, retried the expected tool-only empty response as a visible-answer continuation, and completed without exposing serialized tool-call text as chat output.

What was not tested: Remote Crabbox proof did not complete in this session. Testbox-through-Crabbox failed before checkout with Blacksmith organization access denied. Direct AWS Crabbox failed before lease allocation with an AWS security-group rule quota error. No additional provider families or OS targets were tested beyond the current-head `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` live gateway smoke.

Refs #86924

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unsupported dynamic tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden Codex dynamic-tool registration so malformed runtime tool schemas are diagnosed and quarantined instead of failing assistant turn startup.
- Validate nested JSON Schema shape hazards that Codex rejects, including malformed schema maps, schema arrays, optional schema-valued keywords, `required`, property dependencies, and dynamic schema keywords.
- Preserve valid current-turn executable tools when only stale registered metadata for the same name is invalid.
- Inline local `$ref` schemas under MCP/tool-schema dependency and additional-item keywords so `$defs` are not stripped while dangling refs remain.
- Keep MCP loopback `tools/list` alive when a tool schema union contains malformed property-schema variants; malformed variants are ignored while later valid variants can still define the property.
- Preserve optionality when MCP loopback union schemas include boolean `true` branches instead of over-tightening merged `required` fields.
- Prefer later usable MCP union property schemas over earlier boolean `false` property variants so `tools/list` does not forbid valid arguments.
- Clean Gemini provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency and conditional branches so inspection and normalization agree.
- Clean shared provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency, conditional, tuple, and additional-item branches so xAI-style inspection and normalization agree.
- Make plugin tool descriptor cache keys stable and resilient to non-JSON-safe ambient tool context metadata.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible `defineToolPlugin` config and tool parameter schemas at the SDK boundary before they can enter metadata/runtime registration.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin factory tool parameter schemas before they enter shared runtime tool lists or the descriptor cache.
- Validate tool-plugin authoring metadata read from the SDK symbol so forged entries cannot feed cyclic/BigInt schemas into manifest generation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema validator inputs before recursive schema walking, cache fingerprinting, or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible bundle-MCP draft-2020-12 schemas before recursive schema walking or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema metadata before recursive normalization or metadata exposure.
- Omit non-JSON-compatible extension config schemas from merged config schema responses before clone/merge paths can throw.
- Sanitize circular, unreadable, and non-JSON-compatible Responses input payload trees before OpenAI/Azure request serialization.
- Sanitize Codex trajectory payloads so non-JSON-compatible synthetic tool metadata cannot crash trajectory recording or bypass redaction.
- Reject unreadable plugin host-hook JSON payloads without throwing from host validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex MCP elicitation request/display fields without throwing out of approval bridging.
- Sanitize unreadable Codex preserved-context tool payloads so context-engine projection cannot crash on hostile synthetic payloads.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex app-server approval routing, permission-summary, policy-rewrite, and command payloads.
- Tolerate unreadable Codex app-server protocol response/turn fields before schema validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server notification correlation fields before routing/logging decisions.
- Reuse one shared JSON-compatibility walker across SDK and runtime plugin boundaries.
- Keep test fixtures synthetic (`fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*`) instead of naming the reported third-party plugin.
- Move plugin tool metadata storage into a lightweight module so schema diagnostics can name plugin/tool ownership without importing heavier plugin runtime code.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

target.title: fix(qqbot): validate direct media upload urls
target.body:
## Summary

Validate QQBot direct media upload URLs before they are forwarded to the QQ media upload API, while preserving existing public HTTP(S) direct URL behavior.

## Changes

- Reintroduced direct-upload URL preflight at the `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` sink.
- Requires direct-upload URLs to be valid HTTP or HTTPS URLs.
- Runs the generic SSRF hostname/IP preflight before token lookup or QQ API upload, blocking private/internal/special-use destinations without applying the QQ/Tencent fallback-download allowlist to public direct uploads.
- Added regression coverage for public HTTP(S) URL uploads, invalid/non-HTTP URL rejection, mocked SSRF guard rejection, and real resolver allow/block behavior.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: QQBot direct media URL uploads are validated before the QQ API request body is built. Public HTTP(S) direct URLs remain supported; private/internal/special-use targets are blocked before token lookup or QQ API upload.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout on PR head `549940e47c9afd0e723b3c26ef312dfc1505fd96`. The behavioral proof used the real `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` source and the real SSRF resolver/DNS path, with only the QQ API client and token provider stubbed to avoid credentialed live QQ uploads.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node --import tsx --input-type=module <redacted MediaApi runtime proof script>`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: the non-mocked runtime proof allowed a public HTTPS direct media URL through to the QQ media upload request body, and blocked loopback/private and link-local metadata URL classes with `SsrFBlockedError` before token lookup or client request. The focused Vitest target passed with 1 file and 12 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result after fix: public direct-upload compatibility is preserved for HTTP(S) media URLs, while blocked address classes never request a token and never call `client.request()`.

What was not tested: live QQ API upload with real credentials; broad `check:changed` or full-suite validation.

## Notes

- Security-sensitive exploit details are intentionally omitted from the public PR body.
- AI-assisted.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
- No agent transcript is included.

target.title: Allow validated TXT/JSON/YAML media sends
target.body:
## Summary
- allow host-read local media sends for validated TXT, JSON, YAML, and YML documents
- add YAML MIME/extension mapping for `.yaml` / `.yml`
- keep binary-disguise rejection coverage for the newly allowed text document extensions
- update attachment hydration coverage for the new allowed host-local text document behavior

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** host-read local media sends can attach validated TXT, JSON, YAML, and YML documents while still rejecting binary data disguised with those text extensions.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw checkout on Linux 6.11 / Node v22.22.2, PR head `1b0b35121e5df831217c9003a62fc0a25ef0bee8`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran a live Node/tsx script against `src/media/web-media.ts` that wrote real temporary local files (`note.txt`, `payload.json`, `config.yaml`, `config.yml`, and binary-disguised `evil.json`) and called `loadWebMedia(..., { localRoots: "any", readFile: fs.readFile, hostReadCapability: true })` on each file.
- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the live local-file run:

  ```text
  note.txt: kind=document contentType=text/plain bytes=34
  payload.json: kind=document contentType=application/json bytes=39
  config.yaml: kind=document contentType=application/yaml bytes=33
  config.yml: kind=document contentType=application/yaml bytes=37
  evil.json: rejected binary disguise with code=path-not-allowed
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** real local TXT/JSON/YAML/YML files loaded as `document` media with the expected MIME types; a ZIP-signature binary payload named `evil.json` was rejected with `path-not-allowed`.
- **What was not tested:** no end-to-end delivery to an external chat provider in this proof; this verifies the shared host-read media loader path used before provider send.

## Validation
- `node --import tsx ./.tmp-openclaw-real-media-proof.mjs` (temporary local proof script; output included above)
- `corepack pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.hooks.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.secrets.config.ts` — passed 3 Vitest shards
- `corepack pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts src/media/mime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts` — passed 3 Vitest shards

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31 HEAD_SHORT=14676677a1 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (14676677a1, 14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(discord): accumulate reasoning progress deltas
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83983.

Discord progress-mode reasoning updates were formatting each reasoning callback with the display `Thinking...` wrapper before draft-preview merge logic saw it. Since the merge logic treats `Thinking...`/`Reasoning:` prefixed text as a full snapshot, Codex app-server delta chunks like `"Considering"`, `" plugin"`, `" installation"`, `"!"` could replace each other until only the final tiny chunk remained visible.

This keeps reasoning stream payloads raw until `pushReasoningProgress()` merges delta/snapshot semantics, then applies the existing reasoning display formatter once for the stable progress line.

No config surface or plugin surface changes.

## Proof

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts --reporter=dot
# Test Files 1 passed (1)
# Tests 77 passed (77)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.test.ts --reporter=dot
# Test Files 2 passed (2)
# Tests 117 passed (117)

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.draft-preview.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
# Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

pnpm check:changed
# passed
```

target.title: fix(qqbot): validate direct media upload urls
target.body:
## Summary

Validate QQBot direct media upload URLs before they are forwarded to the QQ media upload API, while preserving existing public HTTP(S) direct URL behavior.

## Changes

- Reintroduced direct-upload URL preflight at the `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` sink.
- Requires direct-upload URLs to be valid HTTP or HTTPS URLs.
- Runs the generic SSRF hostname/IP preflight before token lookup or QQ API upload, blocking private/internal/special-use destinations without applying the QQ/Tencent fallback-download allowlist to public direct uploads.
- Added regression coverage for public HTTP(S) URL uploads, invalid/non-HTTP URL rejection, mocked SSRF guard rejection, and real resolver allow/block behavior.

## Validation

Behavior addressed: QQBot direct media URL uploads are validated before the QQ API request body is built. Public HTTP(S) direct URLs remain supported; private/internal/special-use targets are blocked before token lookup or QQ API upload.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout on PR head `549940e47c9afd0e723b3c26ef312dfc1505fd96`. The behavioral proof used the real `MediaApi.uploadMedia()` source and the real SSRF resolver/DNS path, with only the QQ API client and token provider stubbed to avoid credentialed live QQ uploads.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node --import tsx --input-type=module <redacted MediaApi runtime proof script>`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/api/media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: the non-mocked runtime proof allowed a public HTTPS direct media URL through to the QQ media upload request body, and blocked loopback/private and link-local metadata URL classes with `SsrFBlockedError` before token lookup or client request. The focused Vitest target passed with 1 file and 12 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result after fix: public direct-upload compatibility is preserved for HTTP(S) media URLs, while blocked address classes never request a token and never call `client.request()`.

What was not tested: live QQ API upload with real credentials; broad `check:changed` or full-suite validation.

## Notes

- Security-sensitive exploit details are intentionally omitted from the public PR body.
- AI-assisted.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.
- No agent transcript is included.

target.title: fix(usage): return all agent sessions when no agentId specified
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87132.

When no  is specified in the  request, the backend previously defaulted to returning only the default agent's sessions (usually "main"). This caused the Web UI's agent filter to only show main agent sessions, making it impossible to filter by other agents.

## Changes

- When  is omitted and no specific session key is provided,  is now  instead of defaulting to the configured default agent
- When  is undefined, skip the  step so all agents' sessions are returned
- Updated test to verify that sessions from all configured agents are returned when no agentId is specified

## Test Plan

- Updated existing test to expect all agent sessions returned when no agentId is specified
- Verified existing tests for specific agentId filtering still pass

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping through the shared speech-core preparation path before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.
- Reviewer focus: config schema/docs, the shared TTS preprocessing path, and the empty-after-strip guard.

Out of scope: changing default TTS behavior, validating every vendor-specific pronunciation path, or adding provider-specific filtering.

## Linked context

- Related: #77969.
- This was opened as a focused external fix for noisy TTS output when emoji and decorative symbols are spoken by providers.
- No maintainer-only deployment, credential, or production environment change is required.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch tts-skip-v3, commit 06545b841d08, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  - pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
- Evidence after fix:

      $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
      # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
      # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
      TS
      {
        "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
        "commit": "06545b841d08",
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
        "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
        "observed": [
          {
            "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
            "bytes": 39456,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          },
          {
            "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
            "bytes": 36288,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          }
        ],
        "emptyAfterStrip": {
          "success": false,
          "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
          "provider": null
        },
        "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
      }

      $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
      ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

      $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       32 passed (32)

      $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       14 passed (14)

      $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
      exit_code=0

- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing ✓, ©, and 🫠; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: ffprobe was not installed in the proof container, so artifact validation used nonzero byte sizes and MP3 frame headers. The live proof used the credential-free Microsoft path rather than paid or account-bound providers.
- Before evidence: not captured in this PR body; the regression was that emoji/symbol text reached synthesis without a shared opt-in strip step and empty-after-strip requests could still reach provider setup paths.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for the behavioral change:

    node --import tsx - <<'TS' ...real Microsoft speech-provider proof...
    ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
    pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod

Regression coverage added or updated:

- extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
- src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts

Locked-in scenarios:

- file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
- stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
- telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

Template repair validation on 2026-05-27:

    Authenticated GitHub API read of the PR base template and PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API update of this PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API re-read verified these current template headings are present:
    Summary, Linked context, Real behavior proof (required for external PRs), Tests and validation, Risk checklist, Current review state.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, but only when messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols is explicitly enabled.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: Yes, a new optional config field is added; no migration is needed and unset/false preserves existing behavior.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: Unicode filtering might remove characters a user expected to hear, or filtering might diverge between TTS paths.
- Mitigation: the option is disabled by default, filtering is centralized in the shared speech-core preparation path, and regression tests cover file, stream, telephony, empty-after-strip, and adjacent tool behavior.

Compatibility notes:

- Backward compatible: Yes.
- Config/env changes: Yes, optional messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols.
- Migration needed: No.
- Upgrade steps: none unless a user wants to opt into emoji/symbol stripping.

## Current review state

- Next action: resolve the separate upstream/main generated config metadata conflicts for this branch, then let maintainers and automation continue review.
- Still waiting on author/maintainer/CI/external proof: author-side branch conflict refresh remains tracked separately; no additional external proof is being added in this body-only update.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this body update repairs the current-template gap by replacing the older long-form PR body headings with every current upstream template heading and concrete content under each one.

target.title: fix: stabilize isolated cron prompt cache affinity
target.body:
Fixes #62809

## Summary
- Add a stable hashed prompt-cache affinity key for embedded isolated cron runs.
- Thread that key through the embedded runner into OpenClaw-managed OpenAI transports.
- Preserve fresh isolated run session ids and existing session-bound and CLI cron behavior.

## AI assistance disclosure
AI assistance was used. I reviewed and understand the affected cron executor, embedded runner, stream-resolution, and OpenAI transport paths.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: recurring isolated cron runs now keep stable OpenAI prompt-cache affinity while preserving fresh run sessions.

Real environment tested: local source checkout, actual OpenAI transport parameter builders, no live credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

with a small script invoking `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` and `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` using a sanitized cron-style `promptCacheKey` plus a distinct run `sessionId`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
responses.prompt_cache_key=openclaw:cron:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef
responses.cache_disabled.prompt_cache_key=absent
completions.prompt_cache_key=openclaw:cron:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef
session_identity_still_separate=true
```

Observed result after fix: OpenAI Responses and opted-in OpenAI-compatible Completions payloads use the stable cron prompt-cache key, while `cacheRetention: "none"` omits `prompt_cache_key` and the run session identity remains separate.

What was not tested: live OpenAI cache-hit telemetry was not exercised because it would require real provider credentials and billing-side cache observations. Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was also blocked because the local Crabbox wrapper could not find a usable `crabbox` binary.

## Root Cause
Isolated cron deliberately rotates the transcript/session identity for each run so recurring jobs do not accumulate an ever-growing transcript. The OpenAI payload builders used `sessionId` as the `prompt_cache_key` fallback, so the same recurring isolated cron job also rotated its prompt-cache affinity across runs.

## Dependency contract
OpenAI's SDK exposes `prompt_cache_key` on Responses and Chat Completions request params as the cache-affinity field for similar requests. The local `@earendil-works/pi-ai@0.75.5` stream options expose `sessionId` and `cacheRetention`, but no first-class `promptCacheKey`; its OpenAI providers use `sessionId` for `prompt_cache_key` when prompt caching is enabled. OpenClaw therefore needs a separate internal option so cache affinity can be stable without changing session semantics.

## Regression Test Plan
Focused tests cover:

- isolated cron embedded runs get a stable hashed prompt-cache key across fresh run sessions
- session-bound cron and CLI cron do not receive the embedded prompt-cache key
- `runEmbeddedPiAgent` forwards the key into the embedded attempt
- stream resolution propagates the key without overwriting caller-supplied values
- OpenAI Responses and opted-in Completions prefer `promptCacheKey` over `sessionId`, and omit it when caching is disabled

## Security Impact
No credentials, lockfiles, config files, or secret-handling paths changed. The upstream cache key is derived from stable cron/provider/model identity but sent as a truncated SHA-256 digest, so raw cron names, local session labels, and per-run session ids are not exposed in `prompt_cache_key`.

## Human Verification
I reviewed the affected runtime path from cron execution through embedded runner stream resolution and the OpenAI transport builders, then rechecked the diff against issue #62809 after focused validation.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review
`.github/CODEOWNERS` only names `src/cron/stagger.ts` and `src/cron/service/jobs.ts` under cron. Neither file is touched, so no direct CODEOWNERS owner gate was found for this diff.

## Changelog
No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a normal contributor bugfix PR.

## Review conversations
`codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues after the final patch.

## Risks
Live OpenAI cache-hit telemetry was not tested. The local proof validates the outgoing request payload contract, not OpenAI's server-side cache accounting.

## Behavior tradeoffs
The patch keeps transcript/session isolation and transport session identity separate from prompt-cache affinity. That means recurring isolated cron jobs can share cache buckets across runs without sharing a transcript.

## Scope boundaries
This targets OpenClaw-managed embedded isolated cron OpenAI transport paths. It does not change session-bound cron jobs, CLI cron execution, prompt-prefix construction, source-dependent tool ordering, or arbitrary custom stream implementations that do not use OpenClaw's managed OpenAI transports.

## Validation
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests       469 passed (469)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed

codex review --base origin/main
No actionable correctness issues found
```

Blocked broad validation:

```text
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm check:changed
```

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched (shared gate functions — model-capability resolution, OAuth label, credential fingerprint, live-agent probes, model alias detection — extended via a single `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` helper to recognise both backend ids; every gate continues to return identical values for `claude-cli`). `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Update SDK runtime docs so the public plugin-state contract matches the new 3000-row cap and current-namespace eviction behavior.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.
- Repair the current msteams shrinkwrap guard by pinning `lru-cache` to the `pnpm-lock.yaml` version, so the rebased PR can clear `check-guards`.

## Verification

Latest local checks below were run on rebased head `4b558cf2b4` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md extensions/msteams/package.json`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- Runtime plugin-state probe with the real SQLite store at the 3000-row Telegram shape
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- Full `check-guards` command set, including `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`

Earlier before the final rebase, the focused plugin-state unit suite and Telegram message-cache suite also passed on the same PR diff: `23 passed` and `16 passed` respectively.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on rebased PR head `4b558cf2b4`, existing system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API, and the real plugin-state SQLite store.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows in a temporary plugin-state SQLite DB, opened the real Telegram message-cache keyed store with `maxEntries: 3000`, then registered one new message-cache key `msg-new`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal output from the real runtime probe:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=4b558cf2b4
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=610304
```

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows. The new Telegram message-cache row succeeds, exactly one older message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 topic-name-cache rows remain present, and total live rows stay within the 3000-row plugin cap.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

target.title: refactor: move runtime state to SQLite
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reopens the database-first runtime-state refactor after the accidental direct landing in #78595 was reverted from `main`. It moves active OpenClaw runtime state away from scattered JSON, JSONL, lock files, cache files, and sidecar stores into a typed SQLite layout, while keeping user configuration file-backed.

The main contract is intentionally simple:

- Global control-plane state lives in `~/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
- Per-agent data-plane state lives in `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`.
- `openclaw.json`, plugin manifests, external provider credential files, and user workspaces do not move into SQLite.
- Legacy JSON, JSONL, lock, and sidecar files are doctor migration inputs only. Runtime startup and hot paths should not dual-read or bridge old file state.
- Session/transcript identity is relational: `{ agentId, sessionId }` plus explicit conversation/session metadata. Runtime code must not pass transcript file paths or pseudo-locators such as `sqlite-transcript://...`.

## What changed

- Adds the shared global state database opener, schema, generated Kysely types, transaction helpers, WAL maintenance, permissions handling, and state-lock helpers.
- Adds the per-agent database opener, schema, generated Kysely types, agent database registry rows, and typed session/transcript/VFS/artifact stores.
- Replaces active session, transcript, cron, task, subagent, plugin-state, pairing, diagnostic, media, backup, commitment, delivery queue, and capture stores with SQLite-backed implementations.
- Moves legacy file import/cleanup into `openclaw doctor --fix` style migration surfaces instead of runtime startup compatibility paths.
- Adds database-first guardrails: generated schema checks, Kysely linting, legacy-store scanners, and docs describing the new storage boundary.
- Updates bundled plugins and native apps to use the new state boundaries instead of private file paths where they need durable runtime state.
- Keeps dependency/package-manager/engine/lockfile policy unchanged from `main` for this cleanup PR.

## Architecture

Global database: `state/openclaw.sqlite`

- Owns shared gateway/control-plane state.
- Tracks registered agent databases and global schema metadata.
- Stores auth profile runtime state, pairing/device/node state, plugin state/blob data, scheduler state, task/flow ledgers, delivery queues, backup/migration records, sandbox registry, restart handoff state, diagnostic rows, media blobs, and shared app/plugin runtime state.
- Provides the shared `state_leases` table used by SQLite-backed lock helpers.

Agent database: `agents/<agentId>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`

- Owns agent-local data-plane state.
- Stores sessions, conversation identity, session routing, transcript events, transcript event identities, transcript snapshots, VFS entries, tool/run artifacts, ACP parent stream events, trajectory runtime rows, memory index rows, embedding cache, and generic agent cache entries.
- Lets large per-agent state move out of the shared gateway write lane while still preserving a global registry in `agent_databases`.

Proxy capture database: `src/proxy-capture/schema.sql`

- Keeps the focused capture-store schema for sessions/events/blobs.
- The same capture tables are also present in the global schema for managed state paths that need to live under `openclaw.sqlite`.

## Schema files

- `src/state/openclaw-state-schema.sql`: global control-plane schema, 62 tables.
- `src/state/openclaw-state-schema.generated.ts`: generated SQL module for the global schema.
- `src/state/openclaw-state-db.generated.d.ts`: generated Kysely types for the global schema.
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.sql`: per-agent data-plane schema, 19 tables.
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.generated.ts`: generated SQL module for the per-agent schema.
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.generated.d.ts`: generated Kysely types for the per-agent schema.
- `src/proxy-capture/schema.sql`: standalone proxy capture schema, 3 tables.
- `src/proxy-capture/schema.generated.ts` and `src/proxy-capture/db.generated.d.ts`: generated SQL/types for proxy capture.

Across these schema files there are 84 tables and 110 explicit indexes/unique indexes.

## Global database schema

Core metadata and locking:

- `schema_meta`: database role, schema version, app version, timestamps.
- `agent_databases`: registry of per-agent database paths, schema versions, last-seen timestamps, and size hints.
- `state_leases`: scoped durable lease/lock rows with owner, heartbeat, expiry, and payload.

Auth, diagnostics, and approvals:

- `auth_profile_stores`, `auth_profile_state`: runtime auth profile store/state JSON by store key.
- `diagnostic_events`, `diagnostic_stability_bundles`: scoped diagnostic payloads and generated stability bundles.
- `exec_approvals_config`: persisted exec approval configuration and derived counts.
- `config_health_entries`: last-known-good/promoted config health state.

Pairing, device, node, and native app state:

- `device_pairing_pending`, `device_pairing_paired`: device pairing requests and approved devices.
- `device_bootstrap_tokens`: bootstrap tokens plus issued/redeemed profile metadata.
- `node_pairing_pending`, `node_pairing_paired`: node pairing requests and approved nodes.
- `device_identities`, `device_auth_tokens`: device keypairs and role-scoped tokens.
- `android_notification_recent_packages`: Android notification recent-package ordering.
- `macos_port_guardian_records`: macOS port guardian records.
- `workspace_setup_state`: workspace setup lifecycle state.
- `native_hook_relay_bridges`: native hook relay bridge metadata and expiry.
- `node_host_config`: node-host config state.

Models, media, push, and voice wake:

- `model_capability_cache`: provider/model capability and cost cache.
- `agent_model_catalogs`: generated model catalogs per agent dir.
- `managed_outgoing_image_records`: managed outgoing image attachment metadata.
- `web_push_subscriptions`, `web_push_vapid_keys`: web push subscription and VAPID key state.
- `apns_registrations`: APNS registration rows for native nodes.
- `voicewake_triggers`, `voicewake_routing_config`, `voicewake_routing_routes`: voice wake trigger and routing state.

Plugin and channel state:

- `installed_plugin_index`: persisted installed-plugin index and diagnostics.
- `plugin_state_entries`: JSON plugin state by plugin, namespace, and key.
- `plugin_blob_entries`: binary plugin blobs by plugin, namespace, and key.
- `channel_pairing_requests`, `channel_pairing_allow_entries`: channel pairing codes and allow entries.
- `plugin_binding_approvals`: plugin/channel/account binding approvals.
- `current_conversation_bindings`: current channel conversation bindings to target agent/session rows.

Gateway, replay, delivery, and runtime queues:

- `gateway_restart_sentinel`, `gateway_restart_intent`, `gateway_restart_handoff`: restart continuity and supervisor handoff state.
- `acp_replay_sessions`, `acp_replay_events`: ACP replay session/event ledger.
- `delivery_queue_entries`: durable delivery queue with retry, failure, session, channel, target, and recovery metadata.
- `command_log_entries`: durable command log entries.
- `tui_last_sessions`: last active session per TUI scope.

Tasks, flows, subagents, cron, and commitments:

- `task_runs`: durable task run state.
- `task_delivery_state`: notification state per task.
- `flow_runs`: task-flow/controller state.
- `subagent_runs`: child-agent lifecycle, archive, announcement, frozen-result, and final-delivery state.
- `cron_jobs`: scheduler job definitions plus runtime state.
- `cron_run_logs`: scheduler run history.
- `commitments`: follow-up commitment records and delivery lifecycle.

Storage, backup, migration, sandbox, and capture:

- `media_blobs`: managed media blob storage.
- `skill_uploads`: temporary skill upload archives with idempotency/expiry.
- `sandbox_registry_entries`: sandbox/container registry state.
- `migration_runs`, `migration_sources`: doctor migration run/source ledger.
- `backup_runs`: backup archive manifest records.
- `capture_sessions`, `capture_events`, `capture_blobs`: managed proxy-capture session/event/blob storage.
- `update_check_state`: update notification/auto-install state.

## Per-agent database schema

Session and conversation identity:

- `schema_meta`: agent database role, schema version, agent id, app version, timestamps.
- `sessions`: canonical session metadata, status, channel/account/model metadata, parent/spawn identity, and primary conversation reference.
- `session_routes`: maps stable session keys to canonical session ids.
- `conversations`: typed channel/account/conversation identity with direct/group/channel kind and thread/native ids.
- `session_conversations`: many-to-many session/conversation roles with one primary conversation per session.
- `session_entries`: compatibility/display entry JSON keyed by session key, linked to canonical session id.

Transcripts and trajectory:

- `transcript_events`: append-only transcript event stream keyed by `(session_id, seq)`.
- `transcript_event_identities`: event id, parent/idempotency identity, tail indexes, and event type metadata.
- `transcript_snapshots`: explicit transcript snapshot records with reason/count/metadata.
- `trajectory_runtime_events`: runtime trajectory rows linked to session/run identity.
- `acp_parent_stream_events`: ACP parent stream event ledger by run id and sequence.

Agent-local workspace, artifacts, memory, and cache:

- `vfs_entries`: virtual filesystem namespace/path records with optional blob content and metadata.
- `tool_artifacts`: tool output artifacts by run id and artifact id.
- `run_artifacts`: run artifacts by run id and path.
- `memory_index_meta`: memory index provider/model/config metadata.
- `memory_index_sources`: indexed memory/session sources.
- `memory_index_chunks`: indexed text chunks with embedding blobs and source/session references.
- `memory_embedding_cache`: provider/model/provider-key/hash embedding cache.
- `cache_entries`: generic agent-local cache rows with JSON/blob payloads and optional expiry.

## Runtime contract changes reviewers should focus on

- Runtime code should pass `{ agentId, sessionId }` or `{ agentId, sessionKey }`, not transcript file paths.
- `sessions.session_scope`, `sessions.primary_conversation_id`, `conversations`, and `session_conversations` are the typed routing source of truth. `session_key` remains a routing/display key, not a parsing surface for hidden provider identity.
- Active transcript read/write paths should use `transcript_events` and `transcript_event_identities`; JSONL transcript helpers should be limited to doctor import/export/debug materialization.
- Legacy `sessions.json`, `.jsonl`, `.jsonl.lock`, pruning/truncation files, auth profile files, model catalog files, and old sidecar stores should appear only in doctor migration, backup/import, or explicit legacy tests.
- SQLite schema changes must be reflected in generated SQL modules and Kysely database types.
- Kysely is the expected typed query boundary. Raw SQLite use should stay in low-level openers, transaction helpers, generated-schema plumbing, or deliberately tiny runtime wrappers.

## Important non-goals

- This does not move `openclaw.json` into SQLite.
- This does not move plugin manifests or user workspaces into SQLite.
- This does not introduce runtime dual-read compatibility for old file state.
- This does not add shipped database migrations for the new layout; schema version remains `1` because this layout has not shipped yet.
- This does not change dependency/package-manager/engine policy from `main`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: database-first SQLite runtime refactor is back in a clean review PR after the accidental direct landing was reverted from `main`.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout on Node/pnpm, using a fresh temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` so runtime state is isolated from the maintainer profile.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) pnpm openclaw doctor`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI run after this patch:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp-openclaw-home> pnpm openclaw doctor
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor
[openclaw] Building TypeScript (dist is stale: missing_build_stamp - build stamp missing).
runtime-postbuild: plugin SDK root alias completed in 1ms
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin metadata completed in 137ms
runtime-postbuild: official channel catalog completed in 5ms
runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin runtime overlay completed in 221ms
runtime-postbuild: static extension assets completed in 21ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime imports completed in 136ms
runtime-postbuild: stable root runtime aliases completed in 25ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy root runtime compat aliases completed in 5ms
runtime-postbuild: legacy CLI exit compat chunks completed in 0ms
OpenClaw doctor
State integrity:
- Active state dir: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw
Gateway connection:
- Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
- Source: local loopback
- Config: <temp-openclaw-home>/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Doctor complete.
```

- Observed result after fix: the CLI built and completed the doctor path against the isolated temp state directory; no dependency install or package-manager mutation was needed.
- What was not tested: no broad Docker/live gateway run in this cleanup PR pass.

## Verification

- `pnpm check:database-first-legacy-stores`
- `pnpm db:kysely:check`
- `pnpm lint:kysely`
- `git diff --check && node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- Custom package-manifest proof: dependency/package-manager/engine fields unchanged across 13 changed package manifests.

## Review notes

- The diff is intentionally large because it is the reapply of the reverted database-first branch plus one cleanup commit.
- Commit 1 reapplies the SQLite runtime-state refactor.
- Commit 2 removes a duplicate Docker test script.
- Main revert commit: 694ca50e97 (`Revert "refactor: move runtime state to SQLite"`).
- Supersedes the merged/reverted PR #78595 with a clean reviewable PR.
