target.title: fix(imessage): suppress duplicate native exec approvals
target.body:
## Summary

- Suppresses the local bash-tool `approval-pending` reply for eligible iMessage native exec approvals, so iMessage users see one approval prompt instead of a native prompt plus a fallback `/approve` prompt.
- Moves iMessage pending approval rendering and reaction resolution onto the shared approval reaction SDK helpers, including visible command-action text for plugin approvals.
- Recovers stable Messages GUIDs from `imsg` send results and chat.db lookups so iMessage tapbacks bind to the actual approval prompt.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related milestone: `.mem/main/specs/24-mainstream-channel-exec-plugin-approvals/milestones/24.7-imessage-local-exec-approval-suppression.md`

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes, requested by Kevin in Codex while testing iMessage reaction approvals.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: iMessage exec approvals could double-send: one native reaction approval prompt plus the local bash-tool `approval-pending` fallback prompt.
- Real environment tested: local prod OpenClaw profile, iMessage/SMS route through Google Voice to the configured OpenClaw iMessage chat, approval-required shell exec harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent `Run the shell command exactly: touch /tmp/openclaw-imsg-allow-20260526-233055`, reacted 👍 to the iMessage approval prompt, then repeated with `touch /tmp/openclaw-imsg-deny-20260526-233055` and reacted 👎.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted runtime logs showed `imessage: approval reaction resolved id=f6d91894-9285-49a2-8c26-a3a7f8bfed21 ... decision=allow-once via messageId=F364C7BA-E351-4377-9242-582DB4B77BCC` and `imessage: approval reaction resolved id=979e4e9d-69fc-4000-91e2-3c7ebc6523a5 ... decision=deny via messageId=54F5C126-CF8A-4410-BE94-3397A517EEBE`; screenshots were captured locally at `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-allow-proof.png` and `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-deny-proof.png`.
- Observed result after fix: each command produced exactly one visible iMessage approval prompt; 👍 resolved allow-once and created `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-allow-20260526-233055`; 👎 resolved deny and `/tmp/openclaw-imsg-deny-20260526-233055` remained absent.
- What was not tested: a live ♾️ iMessage tapback path; SDK/unit coverage verifies the binding and iMessage prompt rendering includes manual `/approve` fallback text.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof uses Kevin's local Messages/Google Voice setup; phone numbers and route details are redacted from this PR body.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior repro showed iMessage receiving both the native reaction prompt and the generic local `/approve` fallback for one exec request.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/test-plugin.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/test-plugin.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- iMessage local exec prompt suppression gating and fail-open coverage.
- iMessage reaction binding, target lookup, allow-always reaction, and authorized `is_from_me` tapback behavior.
- iMessage send GUID recovery for numeric ROWIDs and fallback sent-status paths.
- Plugin SDK reaction prompt coverage for plugin command actions.
- Operator approval runtime scope coverage for `operator.write`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Real iMessage exec approval requests could show duplicate approval replies and iMessage tapbacks could fail to bind when the send path returned a numeric row id or no stable GUID.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, approval resolution now gives the operator approval runtime client `operator.write` so native handlers can send resolution/fallback notices after approval decisions.

What is the highest-risk area?

Suppressing the local exec fallback when native iMessage delivery is not actually eligible.

How is that risk mitigated?

The suppressor is metadata-based and fails open unless the approval-pending hint is active, top-level exec approvals are enabled, the native route is active, and the iMessage session route is direct or has explicit approvers. Focused tests cover disabled config, target-only mode, inactive native route, plugin payloads, missing metadata, group sessions without approvers, ambiguous session keys, and agent/session filter mismatches.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review and wait for CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI is pending after PR creation.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

N/A.

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): Fix and unify Chinese translation
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem**: The zh-CN translation file had 3 issues: (1) new panel fields were untranslated (English fallback), (2) manually-reviewed translations from the previous PR were overwritten by the CI locale sync script, (3) terminology was inconsistent — / were translated differently across contexts, and some strings had obvious machine-translation quality. `activity` `delivery` `jitter` `stagger` `Dreaming` - **Why now**: The activity panel feature was recently added upstream with 27 new i18n keys. Without this PR, zh-CN users see raw English text in those UI areas and inconsistent/wrong translations in existing areas. - **Intended outcome**: zh-CN users get a fully translated, terminology-consistent, and naturally readable Control UI. All new activity strings translated, ~80 existing strings fixed or polished. - **Out of scope**: Other locale files (only zh-CN touched). Structural changes to the i18n system. Adding/modifying English source strings. RTL locale completeness (only format fixed for ar/fa). - **Success**: All 27 activity keys show Chinese text. Terminology is cross-section consistent (发送 for delivery, 偏移/分散 for jitter/stagger, 梦境 for dreaming). No English residuals in translated strings. passes clean.`pnpm run control-ui-i18n` ## Linked context - Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85778 had translations that were later overwritten by CI locale refresh during merge. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior addressed**: zh-CN UI translation accuracy and completeness. - **Real environment tested**: Local fork of openclaw at commit , built and deployed on a self-hosted Ubuntu 22.04 VM (OpenClaw Gateway v2026.5.20), viewed in Edge browser.`4f1cd8eb` - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ``` cd ui pnpm build # vite build → ✓ 1.40s pnpm run control-ui-i18n # locale check cp -r dist/control-ui/ /control-ui/ openclaw gateway --port 18789 # start → serves updated UI ``` - **Evidence after fix**: ``` zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0) ``` The 27 fallbacks are pre-existing keys awaiting upstream AI provider validation (no local AI provider configured). All new activity keys are translated and the check detects no issues. - **Observed result**: All changed/dirty keys pass the sync check. No regression in other pending locales. File changed: 1 file, +105/−106 lines. Gateway serves Control UI at with zh-CN rendering correctly.`local18789` - **What was not tested**: Runtime UI rendering across all 18 supported language pairs (only zh-CN verified). Mobile/tablet viewport rendering. - **Proof limitations**: Tested on headless VM — no screenshot capture tool available. Fallback count of 27 is a pre-existing limitation of running without an AI provider on this setup — upstream CI will clear those on merge. - **Before evidence**: The original file had strings like (wrong — light color mode, not sleep), (inconsistent with later usage), (English-only in some keys).`common.light: "浅睡"``cron.runs.delivery: "投递"``dreaming.*: "Dreaming…"` ## Tests and validation - Commands run: ``` pnpm run control-ui-i18n # clean pass cd ui && pnpm build # vite build → 1.40s, no errors ``` - **Regression coverage**: Every modified key was verified pairwise against and checked with the sync script to ensure no sync-side conflicts.`en.ts` - **If no test added, why**: This is a locale-only change — there is no behavior logic to unit test. The i18n check script is the appropriate validation. ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change?** — zh-CN locale text changes. No behavioral or logic changes.`Yes` - **Config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No` - **Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No` - **Highest-risk area**: None. Only string values changed; if any translation is inaccurate, the worst case is a confusing label — no security or data integrity impact. - **Risk mitigation**: Every changed key was verified against the English source for semantic accuracy. Consistent glossary terms were applied across sections. The locale check script confirmed no structural issues.

target.title: fix: stabilize isolated cron prompt cache affinity
target.body:
Fixes #62809

## Summary
- Add a stable hashed prompt-cache affinity key for embedded isolated cron runs.
- Thread that key through the embedded runner into OpenClaw-managed OpenAI transports.
- Preserve fresh isolated run session ids and existing session-bound and CLI cron behavior.

## AI assistance disclosure
AI assistance was used. I reviewed and understand the affected cron executor, embedded runner, stream-resolution, and OpenAI transport paths.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: recurring isolated cron runs now keep stable OpenAI prompt-cache affinity while preserving fresh run sessions.

Real environment tested: local source checkout, actual OpenAI transport parameter builders, no live credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

with a small script invoking `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` and `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` using a sanitized cron-style `promptCacheKey` plus a distinct run `sessionId`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
responses.prompt_cache_key=openclaw:cron:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef
responses.cache_disabled.prompt_cache_key=absent
completions.prompt_cache_key=openclaw:cron:0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef
session_identity_still_separate=true
```

Observed result after fix: OpenAI Responses and opted-in OpenAI-compatible Completions payloads use the stable cron prompt-cache key, while `cacheRetention: "none"` omits `prompt_cache_key` and the run session identity remains separate.

What was not tested: live OpenAI cache-hit telemetry was not exercised because it would require real provider credentials and billing-side cache observations. Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was also blocked because the local Crabbox wrapper could not find a usable `crabbox` binary.

## Root Cause
Isolated cron deliberately rotates the transcript/session identity for each run so recurring jobs do not accumulate an ever-growing transcript. The OpenAI payload builders used `sessionId` as the `prompt_cache_key` fallback, so the same recurring isolated cron job also rotated its prompt-cache affinity across runs.

## Dependency contract
OpenAI's SDK exposes `prompt_cache_key` on Responses and Chat Completions request params as the cache-affinity field for similar requests. The local `@earendil-works/pi-ai@0.75.5` stream options expose `sessionId` and `cacheRetention`, but no first-class `promptCacheKey`; its OpenAI providers use `sessionId` for `prompt_cache_key` when prompt caching is enabled. OpenClaw therefore needs a separate internal option so cache affinity can be stable without changing session semantics.

## Regression Test Plan
Focused tests cover:

- isolated cron embedded runs get a stable hashed prompt-cache key across fresh run sessions
- session-bound cron and CLI cron do not receive the embedded prompt-cache key
- `runEmbeddedPiAgent` forwards the key into the embedded attempt
- stream resolution propagates the key without overwriting caller-supplied values
- OpenAI Responses and opted-in Completions prefer `promptCacheKey` over `sessionId`, and omit it when caching is disabled

## Security Impact
No credentials, lockfiles, config files, or secret-handling paths changed. The upstream cache key is derived from stable cron/provider/model identity but sent as a truncated SHA-256 digest, so raw cron names, local session labels, and per-run session ids are not exposed in `prompt_cache_key`.

## Human Verification
I reviewed the affected runtime path from cron execution through embedded runner stream resolution and the OpenAI transport builders, then rechecked the diff against issue #62809 after focused validation.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review
`.github/CODEOWNERS` only names `src/cron/stagger.ts` and `src/cron/service/jobs.ts` under cron. Neither file is touched, so no direct CODEOWNERS owner gate was found for this diff.

## Changelog
No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a normal contributor bugfix PR.

## Review conversations
`codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues after the final patch.

## Risks
Live OpenAI cache-hit telemetry was not tested. The local proof validates the outgoing request payload contract, not OpenAI's server-side cache accounting.

## Behavior tradeoffs
The patch keeps transcript/session isolation and transport session identity separate from prompt-cache affinity. That means recurring isolated cron jobs can share cache buckets across runs without sharing a transcript.

## Scope boundaries
This targets OpenClaw-managed embedded isolated cron OpenAI transport paths. It does not change session-bound cron jobs, CLI cron execution, prompt-prefix construction, source-dependent tool ordering, or arbitrary custom stream implementations that do not use OpenClaw's managed OpenAI transports.

## Validation
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests       469 passed (469)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/prompt-cache-retention.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/stream-resolution.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
passed

codex review --base origin/main
No actionable correctness issues found
```

Blocked broad validation:

```text
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm check:changed
[crabbox] selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks
```

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): Fix and unify Chinese translation
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem**: The zh-CN translation file had 3 issues: (1) new panel fields were untranslated (English fallback), (2) manually-reviewed translations from the previous PR were overwritten by the CI locale sync script, (3) terminology was inconsistent — / were translated differently across contexts, and some strings had obvious machine-translation quality. `activity` `delivery` `jitter` `stagger` `Dreaming` - **Why now**: The activity panel feature was recently added upstream with 27 new i18n keys. Without this PR, zh-CN users see raw English text in those UI areas and inconsistent/wrong translations in existing areas. - **Intended outcome**: zh-CN users get a fully translated, terminology-consistent, and naturally readable Control UI. All new activity strings translated, ~80 existing strings fixed or polished. - **Out of scope**: Other locale files (only zh-CN touched). Structural changes to the i18n system. Adding/modifying English source strings. RTL locale completeness (only format fixed for ar/fa). - **Success**: All 27 activity keys show Chinese text. Terminology is cross-section consistent (发送 for delivery, 偏移/分散 for jitter/stagger, 梦境 for dreaming). No English residuals in translated strings. passes clean.`pnpm run control-ui-i18n` ## Linked context - Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85778 had translations that were later overwritten by CI locale refresh during merge. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior addressed**: zh-CN UI translation accuracy and completeness. - **Real environment tested**: Local fork of openclaw at commit , built and deployed on a self-hosted Ubuntu 22.04 VM (OpenClaw Gateway v2026.5.20), viewed in Edge browser.`4f1cd8eb` - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ``` cd ui pnpm build # vite build → ✓ 1.40s pnpm run control-ui-i18n # locale check cp -r dist/control-ui/ /control-ui/ openclaw gateway --port 18789 # start → serves updated UI ``` - **Evidence after fix**: ``` zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0) ``` The 27 fallbacks are pre-existing keys awaiting upstream AI provider validation (no local AI provider configured). All new activity keys are translated and the check detects no issues. - **Observed result**: All changed/dirty keys pass the sync check. No regression in other pending locales. File changed: 1 file, +105/−106 lines. Gateway serves Control UI at with zh-CN rendering correctly.`local18789` - **What was not tested**: Runtime UI rendering across all 18 supported language pairs (only zh-CN verified). Mobile/tablet viewport rendering. - **Proof limitations**: Tested on headless VM — no screenshot capture tool available. Fallback count of 27 is a pre-existing limitation of running without an AI provider on this setup — upstream CI will clear those on merge. - **Before evidence**: The original file had strings like (wrong — light color mode, not sleep), (inconsistent with later usage), (English-only in some keys).`common.light: "浅睡"``cron.runs.delivery: "投递"``dreaming.*: "Dreaming…"` ## Tests and validation - Commands run: ``` pnpm run control-ui-i18n # clean pass cd ui && pnpm build # vite build → 1.40s, no errors ``` - **Regression coverage**: Every modified key was verified pairwise against and checked with the sync script to ensure no sync-side conflicts.`en.ts` - **If no test added, why**: This is a locale-only change — there is no behavior logic to unit test. The i18n check script is the appropriate validation. ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change?** — zh-CN locale text changes. No behavioral or logic changes.`Yes` - **Config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No` - **Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No` - **Highest-risk area**: None. Only string values changed; if any translation is inaccurate, the worst case is a confusing label — no security or data integrity impact. - **Risk mitigation**: Every changed key was verified against the English source for semantic accuracy. Consistent glossary terms were applied across sections. The locale check script confirmed no structural issues.

target.title: fix(agent-job): surface pending error snapshot when outer timeout fires
target.body:
### What

Fixes a race condition in `waitForAgentJob` that caused pending agent errors to be discarded when the primary timeout fired.

### Why

When an agent job fails, an `error` event schedules the job to finish with an error snapshot after a 15-second grace period. If the outer `timeoutMs` for `waitForAgentJob` fires before this grace period elapses, it calls `finish(null)`, canceling the pending error.

The caller receives a generic timeout message instead of the actual root cause, making debugging difficult.

### How

The outer timeout callback now checks for a pending error via `getPendingAgentRunError()` before resolving with `null`. This function reads from a module-level map that is populated as soon as the `error` event is received, guaranteeing that the snapshot is available if the race occurs.

A new regression test using fake timers (`5s < 15s`) confirms the fix.

Closes #52497

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Race condition in `waitForAgentJob` — outer timeout fires `finish(null)`, discarding the pending error snapshot. Caller gets a generic timeout instead of the actual agent failure reason.

**Real environment tested:** Local openclaw/openclaw checkout, Node 24.x, WSL2 Ubuntu. Gateway `waitForAgentJob` path exercised directly using fake timers.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**
```
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**Observed result:** All 61 tests pass. The new regression test (`surfaces pending error snapshot when outer timeout fires before error grace period`) passed and asserts that the pending error snapshot is returned when the outer timeout fires before the error grace period.

**What was not tested:** Race with sub-100ms timeouts; concurrent multi-job error races; production OpenClaw load; error snapshots arriving after the outer timeout resolves.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Update SDK runtime docs so the public plugin-state contract matches the new 3000-row cap and current-namespace eviction behavior.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.
- Repair the current msteams shrinkwrap guard by pinning `lru-cache` to the `pnpm-lock.yaml` version, so the rebased PR can clear `check-guards`.

## Verification

Latest local checks below were run on rebased head `4b558cf2b4` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md extensions/msteams/package.json`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- Runtime plugin-state probe with the real SQLite store at the 3000-row Telegram shape
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- Full `check-guards` command set, including `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`

Earlier before the final rebase, the focused plugin-state unit suite and Telegram message-cache suite also passed on the same PR diff: `23 passed` and `16 passed` respectively.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on rebased PR head `4b558cf2b4`, existing system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API, and the real plugin-state SQLite store.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows in a temporary plugin-state SQLite DB, opened the real Telegram message-cache keyed store with `maxEntries: 3000`, then registered one new message-cache key `msg-new`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal output from the real runtime probe:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=4b558cf2b4
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=610304
```

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows. The new Telegram message-cache row succeeds, exactly one older message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 topic-name-cache rows remain present, and total live rows stay within the 3000-row plugin cap.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

target.title: [Fix] Warm provider auth off main thread
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: gateway provider-auth prewarm could monopolize the main Node event loop during startup, delaying channel handshakes and inbound message handling.
- Solution: move the provider-auth warm path into a worker thread while preserving the prepared auth-state cache contract and cancellation semantics.
- What changed: added a provider-auth worker entry, serialized/published warm snapshots, carried runtime auth-store presence into the worker, and updated startup/reload/logout rewarm call sites.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): model-provider auth semantics, credential persistence, channel startup ordering, and public config defaults are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Closes #85999. The reported 2026.5.22 regression shows provider-auth prewarm taking 58-68s with 36s event-loop stalls on a 2vCPU Linux gateway, causing Discord/Feishu/Telegram startup timeouts and delayed inbound messages.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85999
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: provider-auth warm now executes through a worker-thread runtime path instead of doing the expensive provider/auth sweep on the gateway main event loop.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout and built `dist` output on macOS with Node v24.14.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  node --import tsx -e "import('./src/agents/model-provider-auth.ts').then(async ({warmCurrentProviderAuthStateOffMainThread, clearCurrentProviderAuthState}) => { await warmCurrentProviderAuthStateOffMainThread({models:{providers:{demo:{baseUrl:'https://example.com/v1', api:'openai', models:[{id:'m'}]}}}}); clearCurrentProviderAuthState(); console.log('ok-default'); })"
  node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const f=fs.readdirSync('dist').find(f=>/^model-provider-auth-.*\.js$/.test(f)); import('./dist/'+f).then(async m=>{await m.a({models:{providers:{demo:{baseUrl:'https://example.com/v1',api:'openai',models:[{id:'m'}]}}}}, {timeoutMs:30000}); m.n(); console.log('ok', f);})"
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  ok-default
  node --import tsx -e ... 47.43s user 3.84s system 102% cpu 50.221 total

  ok model-provider-auth-OLG_yXSX.js
  ```
- Observed result after fix: both the source-checkout worker path and built dist worker chunk completed successfully and published/cleared provider-auth state without touching channel code.
- What was not tested: the original Azure B2als_v2 host and live Discord/Telegram/Feishu restart path were not available in this local session.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue logs show `provider auth state pre-warmed in 58655ms eventLoopMax=36540.8ms` and `provider auth state pre-warmed in 67840ms eventLoopMax=36876.3ms` on 2026.5.22.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `warmCurrentProviderAuthState` ran the catalog/provider/agent auth discovery sweep on the gateway process main thread after gateway ready. On constrained hosts, external auth discovery and store hydration could stall the event loop long enough to break channel startup timers.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage asserted auth correctness but did not lock the gateway startup/reload/logout call sites to a non-main-thread warm path or require the worker entry in dist/release checks.
- Contributing context (if known): configured agents with multiple providers amplify the per-agent provider-auth sweep.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts`, `src/agents/model-provider-auth.worker.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`, `src/infra/tsdown-config.test.ts`, `test/release-check.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: worker snapshot publication, stale/cancelled warm suppression, runtime auth-store presence transfer, gateway startup/reload/logout using the off-main-thread warmer, and release/build inclusion of the worker entry.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the ownership boundary where the regression entered without requiring live provider credentials or channel transports.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing auth-state tests covered correctness but not the worker boundary.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A - new tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway startup, reload, and logout-triggered provider-auth rewarm should no longer block channel timers on the main event loop. Auth answers and config semantics remain unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
gateway ready -> main-thread provider auth sweep -> channel timers delayed

After:
gateway ready -> worker-thread provider auth sweep -> snapshot publish -> channel timers keep running
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: runtime auth-store snapshots passed to the worker are reduced to provider-presence-only placeholder credentials, so the worker can preserve auth availability semantics without copying real token material.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node v24.14.0, pnpm workspace scripts, built `dist`
- Model/provider: minimal demo OpenAI-compatible provider config
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A - worker/auth runtime path only
- Relevant config (redacted): one demo provider with one model and no secrets

### Steps

1. Run the focused Vitest set for the touched auth/gateway/release surfaces.
2. Build tsdown dist with the worker entry included.
3. Execute the source-checkout worker warm smoke.
4. Execute the built-dist worker warm smoke.
5. Run pre-ship profile scans.

### Expected

- Provider-auth warm completes through the worker path, the built worker chunk is resolvable, and focused tests pass.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed: 11 files, 214 tests.
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs --no-clean` completed successfully.
- Source worker smoke printed `ok-default`.
- Dist worker smoke printed `ok model-provider-auth-OLG_yXSX.js`.
- Pre-ship codex-style, coderabbit-style, and greptileai-style scans returned `NO_FINDINGS`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused auth/gateway/release tests, tsdown build, source worker smoke, built dist worker smoke, pre-ship profile scan.
- Edge cases checked: stale warm cancellation, worker timeout/error handling, invalid worker payloads, runtime auth-store presence transfer, gateway startup/reload/logout call-site rewiring, release-check worker inclusion.
- What you did **not** verify: live affected Linux VM restart and live channel handshakes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A - no migration needed.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: worker startup failures could leave the prepared auth cache cold.
  - Mitigation: failures are logged by existing call sites and `hasAuthForModelProvider` keeps the existing compute fallback when no prepared snapshot matches.
- Risk: runtime-only auth snapshots could leak credential material into worker data.
  - Mitigation: the warm path sends provider-presence-only placeholder credentials, not real token values.

## Summary
- update the Codex Computer Use Quick setup example to set `plugins.entries.codex.config.computerUse.enabled` to `true`

- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(inbound-meta): raise reply context body truncation cap from 500 to 8000 chars
target.body:
## Summary

When a user replies to a bot message, the prior message body is injected into the next turn as `[reply target]` content. That body is sanitized by `sanitizeTranscriptField`, which caps at `MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_FIELD_CHARS = 500` characters in `src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts`. The same 500-char cap applies to both short metadata fields (`sender`, `message_id`, `media_type`, `media_ref`) and long-form body content.

For multi-paragraph bot replies (analyses, option lists, "should I do X?" prompts) the body gets sliced mid-sentence, often before the actionable content. The next turn sees only the preamble and either asks for context or hallucinates from the surviving prefix. This compounds with idle session resets, where the reply context is the only surviving signal.

**Fix:** Split body sanitization from generic field sanitization:

- Add `MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_BODY_CHARS = 8000` for long-form content
- Add `truncateUntrustedTranscriptBody` and `sanitizeTranscriptBody` functions
- Use `sanitizeTranscriptBody` for message body in `formatChatWindowMessage` and for reply quote text in `resolveInlineReplyQuote`
- Short metadata fields (`sender`, `message_id`, `media_type`, `media_ref`) keep the original 500-char cap

Closes #87291

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts` -- all 49 tests pass (5 new)
- `pnpm format:check` -- clean
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` -- 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm build` -- successful; new body cap confirmed in production bundle

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Reply context body (chat window messages and inline reply quotes) is truncated at 500 chars, the same cap used for short metadata fields like sender and message_id. Multi-paragraph bot replies are sliced mid-sentence, hiding actionable content from the next turn (issue #87291).
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source on branch `fix/reply-context-body-truncation`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  git checkout fix/reply-context-body-truncation
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  grep -n "MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_BODY_CHARS\|sanitizeTranscriptBody" dist/get-reply-jvOJ2TTM.js
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build confirms the dedicated body sanitization path is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_BODY_CHARS\|sanitizeTranscriptBody" dist/get-reply-jvOJ2TTM.js
  1858:const MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_BODY_CHARS = 8e3;
  1900:if (value.length <= MAX_UNTRUSTED_TRANSCRIPT_BODY_CHARS) return value;
  1903:function sanitizeTranscriptBody(value) {
  1945:const body = sanitizeTranscriptBody(value["body"]);
  2055:return sanitizeTranscriptBody(ctx.ReplyToQuoteText) ?? sanitizeTranscriptBody(ctx.ReplyToBody);
  ```

  Terminal confirms body content survives past the old 500-char field cap while short metadata fields still truncate correctly:

  ```console
  $ pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts
  ✓ preserves chat window message body past the 500-char field cap
  ✓ truncates chat window message body at the body cap (8000 chars)
  ✓ still truncates short metadata fields at 500 chars in chat window messages
  ✓ preserves reply quote text past the 500-char field cap
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  49 passed (49)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled bundle at `dist/get-reply-jvOJ2TTM.js` routes message body and reply quote text through `sanitizeTranscriptBody` with an 8000-char cap, while short metadata fields continue through `sanitizeTranscriptField` at 500 chars. Terminal confirms 1500-char body content passes through without truncation, 10000-char body is correctly capped, and 600-char sender names are still truncated at the field cap.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram or Slack sessions where a user replies to a bot message after an idle session reset. The fix is on the shared `formatChatWindowMessage` path used by all channels.

target.title: fix(update): preserve newer managed plugin pins
target.body:
`openclaw update` could silently downgrade a user-pinned managed plugin direct dependency, reintroducing plugin hotfix bugs and leaving the managed npm root inconsistent with user intent.

Fixes #85184.

## Affected surface

- `src/plugins/install.ts`
  - `installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot`
  - managed npm update-mode resolution handling
- `src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts`
  - `upsertManagedNpmRootDependency`
  - managed root direct dependency spec reading
- Tests:
  - `src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts`

## Scope

This is a narrow update-path guard for exact semver direct dependency pins. It does not introduce a new config flag and does not change general install-mode behavior.

- In `mode: "update"`, detect when the managed npm root already has a newer exact direct dependency spec than the update target.
- If that newer version is already installed with lockfile metadata, reuse the installed package instead of running `npm install` and downgrading it.
- If the package is pinned newer and installed but lockfile metadata is missing, re-resolve npm metadata for the preserved newer spec before install/verification so integrity and shasum describe the same version.
- If preserved-version npm metadata cannot be resolved, fail closed instead of synthesizing a version-only registry resolution.
- Non-semver specs, equal versions, older pins, manual install mode, and unrelated dependency updates keep the existing behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: After this patch, an `openclaw update`-mode plugin install preserves a user-pinned newer exact managed npm root dependency instead of downgrading it to the older bundled update target. When matching lockfile metadata is missing, the update path re-resolves metadata for the preserved newer version so `version`, `integrity`, and lockfile state stay consistent.
- Real environment tested: Linux local development executor, current PR head `0dc45473f9bc`, real OpenClaw source imported with `node --import tsx`, real managed npm root mutation through `installPluginFromNpmSpec(..., { mode: "update" })`, real npm registry package `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw`, temporary proof directory outside the repository with repository path redacted as `<repo>`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Create a temporary managed npm root outside the repository; install/pin `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2`; request update-mode install for older bundled target `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0`; verify manifest, installed package, and lockfile remain on `0.11.2`; repeat after removing matching lockfile metadata to verify metadata is re-resolved for `0.11.2`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the mutating managed npm root proof command:

```text
OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=0dc45473f9bc node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr87304-proof.mjs
```

Redacted proof setup output:

```json
{
  "proof": "OpenClaw PR #87304 managed npm root update proof",
  "head": "0dc45473f9bc",
  "repository": "<repo>",
  "packageName": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw",
  "olderSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
  "newerSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2"
}
```

Scenario 1: existing newer install with lockfile metadata. The update request was for `0.10.0`, but the managed root kept the user-pinned `0.11.2`.

```json
{
  "scenario": "existing-installed-and-locked",
  "input": {
    "requestedUpdateSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
    "existingPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "existingInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "lockfilePresentBeforeUpdate": true
  },
  "observedResult": {
    "ok": true,
    "resultVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA==",
    "finalPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "finalInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA=="
  }
}
```

Scenario 2: existing newer install but missing lockfile metadata. The update path re-resolved the preserved `0.11.2` metadata, rebuilt lockfile metadata, and still did not downgrade to `0.10.0`.

```json
{
  "scenario": "existing-installed-lockfile-missing",
  "input": {
    "requestedUpdateSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
    "existingPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "existingInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "lockfilePresentBeforeUpdate": false
  },
  "observedResult": {
    "ok": true,
    "resultVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA==",
    "finalPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",

```

```text

Error: npm install resolved @martian-engineering/lossless-claw to version 0.10.0, expected 0.11.2
```

ClawSweeper P2 repair evidence: head `0dc45473f9bc` also adds a fail-closed regression for preserved newer pins when the preserved-version metadata lookup fails. The update now returns an error before `npm install` instead of proceeding with a synthetic version-only resolution.

Local validation after the patch:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  19 passed (19)

(exit 0)

$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
(exit 0)

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

- Observed result after fix: Update-mode install did not downgrade the managed root dependency from `0.11.2` to `0.10.0`. The returned plugin install result, final `package.json` pin, installed package version, `package-lock.json` version, and lockfile integrity all stayed on `0.11.2`. The missing-lockfile-metadata path regenerated metadata for `0.11.2`, avoiding stale integrity. The preserved-metadata failure regression now fails before `npm install`, so the preserved path no longer proceeds with a version-only synthetic registry resolution.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full package-manager `openclaw update --tag` against a real user home directory. I did not run the proof against a live Gateway process; this patch is limited to the managed npm root plugin update/install boundary.

## Coverage note

I re-ran the required no-collision searches immediately before pushing this branch. The only returned PR is this PR, `#87304`; no competing open PR covers `#85184`:

```text

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31 HEAD_SHORT=14676677a1 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (14676677a1, 14676677a1f33b8f8f87f999efb944c4d78f6c31)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: feat(plugins): add optional api.resetSession()
target.body:
## Summary

`main` already has gateway-side session reset support through `performGatewaySessionReset()`.

This PR adds optional plugin-side `api.resetSession(key, reason?)` support on top of the current mainline architecture.

- Adds `PluginResetSessionResult` type and optional `resetSession` on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Wires `api.resetSession(...)` in the plugin registry only in full registration mode and only when the gateway exposes `sessions.reset`
- Reuses gateway-owned canonicalization and reset resolution (no plugin-side duplicate logic)
- Unifies config snapshot usage in `performGatewaySessionReset` to eliminate config-drift between target resolution and entry lookup
- Adds per-canonical-key in-flight guard in the reset service
- Exports `PluginResetSessionResult` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`

## Scope

This PR:
- adds `PluginResetSessionResult` to `src/plugins/types.ts`
- adds optional `resetSession` to `OpenClawPluginApi`
- adds builder support in `src/plugins/api-builder.ts`
- wires `api.resetSession(...)` in `src/plugins/registry.ts` (full-mode + capability gated)
- adds optional `existingCfg` param to `loadSessionEntry` in `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- adds in-flight guard and unified resolution in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`
- exports the result type from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
- adds focused plugin contract tests (17 tests)
- refreshes generated plugin SDK API baselines

This PR does not:
- change gateway reset RPC behavior
- broaden session architecture scope beyond the plugin API surface
- revive any stale-branch refactor baggage from the closed #43375

## Behavior

`api.resetSession(key, reason?)`:
- accepts `"new"` or `"reset"` reason values (defaults to `"new"`)
- trims and validates the key
- delegates to gateway-owned `performGatewaySessionReset`
- resolves to a structured plugin result instead of rejecting for operational failures
- absent in setup-only, setup-runtime, and cli-metadata registration modes
- absent when gateway does not expose `sessions.reset`

Success: `{ ok: true, key, sessionId }`
Failure: `{ ok: false, key, code, message }`

## Local Verification

```
pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts     # 17/17 pass
pnpm test -- src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts      # 77/77 pass
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check                           # OK
pnpm tsgo                                           # no new errors
```

Replaces #43375 (closed, stale branch).

target.title: Harden hostname normalization for repeated trailing dots [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden hostname canonicalization so repeated terminal dots are treated the same as a canonical hostname before network guard policy checks.
- Add regression coverage for blocked hostnames with repeated trailing dots at the normalizer, SSRF policy, and trusted environment proxy dispatch boundary.
- Keep SSRF test helpers on the shared production hostname normalizer to avoid drift in plugin-facing test utilities.
- Keep the change scoped to shared hostname normalization and network guard tests; no config, migration, or public API changes are intended.
- AI-assisted.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No public issue is closed by this PR body.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No public related context is linked here.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No public maintainer request is linked here.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Reserved hostnames with repeated trailing dots are blocked before trusted proxy dispatch.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using the production `fetchWithSsrFGuard` trusted proxy path with `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:7890`; fetch and DNS lookup were instrumented as dispatch canaries.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:7890 ./node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval '<script importing fetchWithSsrFGuard and calling repeated-dot reserved URLs in TRUSTED_ENV_PROXY mode>'`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://localhost... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
BLOCKED http://localhost.../resource :: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://metadata.google.internal... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
BLOCKED http://metadata.google.internal.../computeMetadata/v1/ :: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://api.localhost... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
BLOCKED http://api.localhost.../resource :: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://svc.local... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
BLOCKED http://svc.local.../resource :: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://db.internal... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
BLOCKED http://db.internal.../resource :: Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
DISPATCH_COUNTS fetch=0 lookup=0
```

- Observed result after fix: Repeated-dot variants of `localhost`, `metadata.google.internal`, `.localhost`, `.local`, and `.internal` hostnames are rejected before DNS lookup or fetch dispatch in trusted proxy mode.
- What was not tested: Live forwarding through a real external proxy or access to a cloud metadata service.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof intentionally stops at the guard boundary and uses dispatch canaries to verify no fetch or DNS lookup happens.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing normalization stripped only one trailing dot, leaving repeated-dot hostnames non-canonical for exact and suffix checks.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/net/hostname.test.ts src/infra/net/ssrf.test.ts src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts`

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/net/hostname.test.ts src/infra/net/ssrf.test.ts src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts`

`git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

`node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/net/hostname.ts src/infra/net/hostname.test.ts src/infra/net/ssrf.test.ts src/infra/net/fetch-guard.ssrf.test.ts src/test-helpers/ssrf.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Canonical hostname normalization now covers repeated trailing dots. SSRF policy tests cover blocked reserved hostnames with repeated trailing dots. Trusted proxy guard tests cover rejection before lookup or fetch dispatch. The plugin-facing SSRF test helper now reuses the shared normalizer.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Repeated terminal dots could leave a hostname outside the canonical exact/suffix blocked forms.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, malformed or unusual repeated-dot forms of blocked hostnames are now rejected consistently.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, network hostname guard canonicalization is stricter for repeated trailing dots.

What is the highest-risk area?

Network guard hostname canonicalization is shared by SSRF policy and SDK-exposed helpers.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation is a narrow canonicalization change with focused normalizer, SSRF policy, trusted proxy guard, helper-parity regression coverage, and terminal guard-boundary proof.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await ClawSweeper re-review, CI status, and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

ClawSweeper re-review and maintainer review are pending. CI has an unrelated existing `check-lint` failure in an unchanged file.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper requested real behavior proof; the PR body now includes terminal guard-boundary proof showing repeated-dot reserved hostnames blocked before fetch or DNS lookup.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Update SDK runtime docs so the public plugin-state contract matches the new 3000-row cap and current-namespace eviction behavior.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.
- Repair the current msteams shrinkwrap guard by pinning `lru-cache` to the `pnpm-lock.yaml` version, so the rebased PR can clear `check-guards`.

## Verification

Latest local checks below were run on rebased head `4b558cf2b4` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md extensions/msteams/package.json`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- Runtime plugin-state probe with the real SQLite store at the 3000-row Telegram shape
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- Full `check-guards` command set, including `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`

Earlier before the final rebase, the focused plugin-state unit suite and Telegram message-cache suite also passed on the same PR diff: `23 passed` and `16 passed` respectively.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on rebased PR head `4b558cf2b4`, existing system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API, and the real plugin-state SQLite store.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows in a temporary plugin-state SQLite DB, opened the real Telegram message-cache keyed store with `maxEntries: 3000`, then registered one new message-cache key `msg-new`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal output from the real runtime probe:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=4b558cf2b4
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=610304
```

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows. The new Telegram message-cache row succeeds, exactly one older message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 topic-name-cache rows remain present, and total live rows stay within the 3000-row plugin cap.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

target.title: fix(logging): resolve hostname lazily to avoid caching empty string on macOS
target.body:
## Summary
- Replace module-level `HOSTNAME` constant with lazy `getHostname()` function
- macOS intermittently returns empty hostname during ESM module load phase
- Lazy resolution ensures hostname is captured correctly after gateway startup

## Reproduction
```bash
grep '"hostname"' /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log | head -5
```
Before fix:
```
_meta.hostname: "unknown"   ← wrong
hostname: "hopembpm5deMBP"  ← correct (from different source)
```

## Root Cause
`os.hostname()` returns empty string on macOS during module load, cached as "unknown" for entire gateway lifetime.

Fixes #87258

target.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fix two Windows / provider-compatibility issues:

1. QMD binary resolution on Windows when `memory.qmd.command` resolves to a `.cmd` shim instead of a native executable.
2. OpenAI-compatible image generation responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.

The QMD issue was found with QMD installed via:

```bash
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd
```

In the affected Windows environment, the working `qmd` command was provided by a `.cmd` wrapper like:

```bat
@echo off
node "E:\software\encoding\common\nodeJs\node_global\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js" %*
```

Running this wrapper from the shell worked normally, but OpenClaw's Windows wrapper resolver only handled `%~dp0`-relative shim targets. Quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints were skipped, so QMD availability checks failed closed with:

```text
qmd.CMD wrapper resolved, but no executable/Node entrypoint could be resolved without shell execution
```

This PR allows Windows `.cmd` wrappers to resolve quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints without enabling shell execution.

It also updates QMD package fallback resolution from `qmd` to the actual npm package name, `@tobilu/qmd`.

For image generation, some OpenAI-compatible providers such as NewAPI return generated images as hosted URLs:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "url": "https://provider.example/generated-image.png"
    }
  ]
}
```

OpenClaw previously only parsed `data[].b64_json`, so these responses produced no generated image assets.

This PR adds support for `data[].url` while routing provider-controlled URL downloads through guarded provider HTTP.

## Changes

### QMD / Windows

- Support quoted absolute Windows entrypoints in `.cmd` shim parsing
- Preserve the existing `%~dp0`-relative shim handling
- Apply the same resolver fix to both:
  - plugin SDK Windows spawn resolver
  - memory host SDK Windows spawn resolver
- Use `@tobilu/qmd` for QMD package fallback resolution
- Add unit coverage for `.cmd` shims that quote an absolute JavaScript entrypoint

### OpenAI-compatible image generation

- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that include `data[].url`
- Prefer `data[].b64_json` when both `b64_json` and `url` are present
- Download URL-based image assets and infer MIME/file extension from response headers and image bytes
- Route provider-returned image URL downloads through `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)`
- Preserve provider HTTP guardrails for:
  - timeout
  - SSRF/private-network policy
  - dispatcher/proxy policy
  - audit context
  - guarded response release handling
- Add unit coverage proving URL downloads use guarded provider HTTP instead of raw fetch

## Security

Provider-controlled image URLs are not fetched with raw `fetch`.

The follow-up URL download path uses `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)` and carries the same provider HTTP controls used for image generation requests:

- request timeout
- SSRF policy
- private-network opt-in
- dispatcher/proxy policy
- audit context
- guarded response release handling

This avoids bypassing SSRF/private-network policy, proxy/dispatcher policy, and timeout handling.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: QMD Windows `.cmd` shim resolution for quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints and scoped `@tobilu/qmd` fallback resolution.
- Real environment tested: Windows_NT 10.0.22631, Node v24.9.0, branch `fix/qmd-newapi-real-proof`, repo `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx proof script from `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw` that created a temporary Windows-style `node_modules\.bin\qmd.cmd` wrapper pointing at a quoted absolute `node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli.js` entrypoint, then called `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, and `resolveCliSpawnInvocation(...)`.
- Evidence after fix:
  
  ```json
  {
    "cwd": "E:\\sourceCode\\java\\my\\openclaw\\openclaw",
    "platform": "win32",
    "node": "v24.9.0",
    "shimPath": "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\.bin\\qmd.cmd",
    "shimContainsAbsoluteJsEntrypointWithoutDp0": true,
    "packageName": "@tobilu/qmd",
    "program": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "leadingArgv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "invocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--version"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "cliInvocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--help"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "shellFallbackUsed": false
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The quoted absolute `.cmd` shim entrypoint resolves to direct Node execution of the `@tobilu/qmd` JavaScript entrypoint, and `shellFallbackUsed` is `false`.
- What was not tested: Full live QMD memory indexing/search workflow and macOS were not tested in this proof run. Image URL guarded fetching is covered by targeted unit tests and code inspection rather than this Windows runtime proof.

## Image generation validation

Validated the OpenAI-compatible URL-response parser and guarded download path with targeted tests.

Before this fix, OpenClaw parsed only `data[].b64_json`, causing URL-only image responses to fail with no generated image assets.

After this fix:

- URL-only OpenAI-compatible image responses produce generated image assets
- `b64_json` remains preferred when present
- URL downloads go through guarded provider HTTP instead of raw `fetch`

For the OpenAI-compatible image URL path, I also ran `image_generate` locally through the configured OpenAI-compatible image provider after this patch.
Evidence after fix:
```text
Generated 1 image with openai/gpt-image-2.

Observed result after fix: URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image generation completed successfully and produced a generated image asset in the local OpenClaw setup.


## Testing

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
2 test files passed
10 tests passed
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "resolves bare qmd command through the scoped QMD package fallback on Windows"
```

Result:

```text
1 test passed
```

```bash
pnpm format:check src/image-generation/image-assets.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/windows-spawn.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```bash
git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Result:

```text
passed
```

## Platform notes

The QMD wrapper-resolution change has been validated on Windows.

macOS was not tested. The changed QMD wrapper-resolution path is Windows-only (`platform === "win32"`), and non-Windows platforms continue to use the existing direct command path.

The OpenAI-compatible image response parsing change is platform-independent.
